body.title: Migrate lab/claudecode/* logging to get_logger pattern (excluding archive)
body.result: 4 non-CLI files migrated to get_logger. CLI exemption broadened (suppress all prints in __main__-guarded files). lab/claudecode metrics: print_smell 910→122, logging_bypass 4→1 (cc_bridge bridge-setup documented), legacy_log 52→12 (function-scope refinement deferred). 4119 tests pass.
body.description:
Migrate logging callsites in lab/claudecode/*.py (excluding archive/ — see T-logging-archive-decision). Most lab/claudecode/ scripts are module-level (no class), so primary pattern is get_logger(__name__) replacing print() + logging.getLogger().

Counts from 2026-04-29 audit:
- Many seed_* and migration scripts use print() heavily — these are CLI tools where print() may be acceptable for output (filter via CLI exemption)
- Non-CLI helpers use logging.getLogger or _log: cc_bridge.py (12 _log + 1 bypass), reading_campaign.py, reading_integrator.py, etc.

Gated on T-logging-migrate-utility-closet so the migration pattern (especially the get_logger module-level helper) is proven first.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/*.py excluding archive/. Per-file scope determined by audit punch list.
**Design rules:** inherit-base-class for any classes; get_logger() for module-level.
**Scope boundary:** in scope: lab/claudecode/ excluding archive/. Out of scope: archive/ (T-logging-archive-decision), engram_tools (cover via T-logging-class-inheritance-fixups).
**Test plan:** existing tests pass. Re-run audit_logging.py to verify lab/claudecode/ counts dropped (excluding archive/).
